mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as the economy recovers from a downturn.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.7% suggests a recovery phase is likely
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate profits decline due to reduced demand and increased uncertainty.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.7% indicates significant slack and potential for further deterioration
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a triggered UNRATE increase.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with persistently low federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, which boosts corporate revenues and equity valuations by lowering the cost of capital.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% is very low, suggesting accommodative policy
regime_hint: post-recessionary demand recovery with accommodative policy
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where both conditions for the trigger are met.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low inflation in an environment of very low interest rates suggests that the central bank has room to maintain accommodative policy, which supports equity valuations by lowering discount rates and encouraging risk-taking.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.541 and FEDFUNDS at 0.11% reflect low inflation and rates
regime_hint: low inflation, low interest rate environment
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where both conditions are met, indicating a lack of market response to accommodative policy.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts signals strengthening consumer and business confidence, indicating future economic activity and demand for goods and services, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns as corporate outlook improves.
failure_condition: If HOUST 3-month average growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing and mining sectors, signaling broader economic contraction and reduced corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns due to lower output.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average change is negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a triggered INDPRO decline, suggesting resilience despite industrial weakness.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at or near the zero lower bound indicates highly accommodative monetary policy, which reduces borrowing costs for corporations and encourages investment, supporting equity valuations by making other asset classes less attractive.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% is near zero, indicating ZIRP
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, suggesting ZIRP is ineffective or signals deeper problems.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, reflecting strong aggregate demand and corporate profitability, which typically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence rises.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of periods following a triggered GDPC1 growth signal, indicating a disconnect between economic growth and market performance.
